target.name: Beyond Aero
target.founder: Valéry Manuel, Eloa Guillotin, Hugo Tarlé

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $44M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: REGENT Craft
private.rationale: Same sector (Transportation), same stage (Series A), post-money $270M

data.narrative:
Beyond Aero is a Series A transportation company founded by Valéry Manuel, Eloa Guillotin, and Hugo Tarĺé, which has raised $44M to date. The comparable universe consists of eight private transportation companies including REGENT Craft, Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, and Harbinger, with no public peer set available for direct benchmarking.

A single valuation method proved applicable given the data constraints. Stage-Progression analysis applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement to the $44M capital raised proxy, implying a valuation of $88M. Peer-Median analysis was not feasible; while five same-sector peers exist in Transportation, the narrow filters—same-stage, adjacent-stage, and similar valuation tier—yielded insufficient observations (fewer than three datapoints required for median reliability), making an all-sector median inappropriate and potentially misleading.

The triangulated valuation range is $75M to $101M, with a median point of $88M. This range reflects the methodological constraint of relying on a single applicable approach rather than true triangulation across independent methods. Key uncertainties include the absence of comparable private-market multiples in the immediate peer set and the lack of public market reference points. Stage-Progression multiers are inherently backward-looking and may not capture frontier transportation segment dynamics. Additional data on peer funding rounds, burn profiles, or commercial traction would strengthen conviction in this range.
